class.name: DWM
class.brief_description: Provides access to Windows Desktop Window Manager (DWM) APIs for advanced window effects and customization.
class.description: The [DWM] class allows you to interact with Windows Desktop Window Manager to enable modern Windows 11/10 visual effects such as Mica, Acrylic backdrop materials, custom title bar colors, dark mode, and extend the window frame into client area. This class is only available on Windows platforms and most methods require a [Window] node as target. All methods are static, so you don't need to instantiate the class. [b]Note:[/b] Some features require specific Windows versions. For example, [method set_systembackdrop_type] requires Windows 11 Build 22000 or later for Mica effects. [b]Important:[/b] When changing window characteristics, the window must be visible and have native decorations. and the window is visible before calling DWM methods.

link.title: Custom Window Title Bars in Godot

method.name: enable_dark_mode
method.description: Enables or disables dark mode for the specified window's title bar and borders. This affects the native window decorations when using [member DisplayServer.window_set_flag] with [constant DisplayServer.WINDOW_FLAG_BORDERLESS] set to [code]false[/code]. [codeblock] # Enable dark mode for the main window DWM.enable_dark_mode(get_window(), true) [/codeblock]

method.name: enable_hostbackdropbrush
method.description: Enables or disables the host backdrop brush effect for the window. This is an older DWM effect that allows the window behind to show through. Consider using [method set_systembackdrop_type] for modern alternatives.

method.name: enable_mmcss
method.description: Enables or disables Multimedia Class Scheduler Service (MMCSS) for the current process. This can help improve performance for multimedia applications by prioritizing threads.

method.name: extend_frame_into_client_area
method.description: Extends the window frame into the client area by specified margins. This is useful for creating custom title bars while keeping native window controls. Set margins to [code]-1[/code] to extend fully in that direction. [codeblock] # Extend only the top for a custom title bar DWM.extend_frame_into_client_area(get_window(), 0, 0, 32, 0) [/codeblock]

method.name: get_border_color
method.description: Returns the current border color of the window as a [Color].

method.name: get_colorization_color
method.description: Returns the user's global DWM colorization color (the color used for window borders and taskbar). This respects the user's Windows personalization settings.

method.name: get_composition_timing_info
method.description: Returns timing information about the DWM composition process as a Dictionary. Contains data like refresh rate, composition rate, and frame delays. [b]Note:[/b] This is intended for debugging and performance monitoring purposes.

method.name: get_title_bar_color
method.description: Returns the current title bar color of the window as a [Color].

method.name: is_composition_enabled
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if DWM composition is enabled system-wide. Composition is required for most visual effects.

method.name: is_title_bar_coloring_supported
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the current Windows version supports custom title bar coloring. Always check this before calling color-related methods.

method.name: reset_to_default_colors
method.description: Resets the window's title bar and border colors to system defaults.

method.name: set_border_color
method.description: Sets a custom color for the window border.

method.name: set_systembackdrop_type
method.description: Sets the system backdrop material type for the window. Available options are: - [constant AUTO]: Automatically choose based on window type - [constant NONE]: No backdrop effect - [constant MAINWINDOW]: Mica effect for main windows (Windows 11+) - [constant TRANSIENTWINDOW]: Acrylic effect for transient windows - [constant TABBEDWINDOW]: Mica Alt effect for tabbed interfaces [codeblock] # Apply Mica effect to main window DWM.set_systembackdrop_type(get_window(), DWM.MAINWINDOW) [/codeblock]

method.name: set_title_bar_color
method.description: Sets a custom color for the title bar. If [param sync_border] is [code]true[/code], the border color will be synchronized with the title bar color. [codeblock] # Set a red title bar DWM.set_title_bar_color(get_window(), Color(0.8, 0.1, 0.1)) [/codeblock]

constants.constant: Automatically selects an appropriate backdrop type based on the window's context and type.
No system backdrop effect; uses traditional rendering.
Applies the Mica backdrop material, ideal for main application windows. Available on Windows 11 Build 22000+.
Applies the Acrylic backdrop material, suitable for transient windows like dialogs and flyouts.
Applies the Mica Alt backdrop material, optimized for applications with tabbed interfaces.
